rows.notes: Very clear year at top centre of page in dark ink, unambiguous.
Clear heading directly under the year; lettering legible and centered.
Label appears as a group heading near the top-left of the description column; letters are clear though slightly stylized.

Row is heavily struck through by a horizontal line but the amount '46 14 2' in the three right-hand columns is still legible. The description is partly obscured and partly crossed out; 'Repayment to Bass. subscription' is a best-read of the handwriting but letters are uncertain due to the strike-through.
Description 'Whitehead Interest' is reasonably legible. Amount columns read as '56 2 9' though the topmost two digits are slightly cramped; values are within valid ranges.
Description is faint but readable as 'Drypine divided 2 years not charged' (first word uncertain — could be 'Dupince' or similar). Amount appears clearly as '3 18 0' in the £/s/d columns.
Description 'Watch expense Queen St' is readable. Amount aligned in the columns as '45 2 6' with clear digits.
Text 'Gwinear Tithe Allowance' is legible though the place name is uncommon; amount '46 10 0' is clearly aligned and digits are distinct.
Section label 'Receipts' appears centered beneath the previous entries; letters clear.
The word in parentheses reads '(Commission)'. Amount to the right reads '4 19 0' though the '4' and '19' are small; alignment with the column lines supports this reading.
Clear description 'Tring allowance for fixtures'. Amount appears as '13 0 0' (pounds clearly '13').
Description 'Nurses Home' readable; amount reads '11 19 6' though ink is slightly lighter on the pence digit.
Handwriting 'Thames Valley drainage' clear. Amount '5 5 0' is easy to read and well aligned.

Description is difficult in parts; reads as 'Band Collage (Commn &c)' — could be 'Rand Cottage' or 'Band Collage'. Amount '4 4 5' visible but small; the middle word's letters are uncertain so confidence lowered.
Sub-item written smaller: 'Roby Bill 1.4.5' is directly stated in the description column; amounts correspond to the text and align with columns. Description is relatively clear.
Sub-item 'Brown Do 16.0' legible and the amount '16 0 0' is written inline with the description (interpreted as £16).
Reads 'Gardens (Commn) 5.6' as a sub-item. Amount extracted from the inline notation and appears consistent with the narrow s/d spacing.
Sub-item 'Hill Bill 2.0' clearly written; interpreted as £2 0s 0d.
Sub-item 'Band Bill 13.6' legible; amount interpreted as £13 6s 0d from the inline figure.
Description likely 'Roades draining' (could be 'Roads draining'). Amount appears as '1 10 0' but the pounds digit is small and slightly uncertain due to handwriting.
Handwriting is cramped and the phrase 'Burwar Domus Battles' is hard to parse; amount reads '15 4 0' but letters in the description are uncertain. Could be multiple words run together.
Short entry 'Imprs Coe.' (abbreviation unclear). The amount '85 12 6' is readable though the tens digit is slightly smudged; values within normal ranges.
Description 'Poor Rate returned' decipherable. Amount '61 4 6' appears clearly aligned in the columns.
Word reads 'Percentages' though the handwriting is a bit cramped. Amount '2 18 6' is legible but the '2' pounds is small, reducing confidence slightly.
Entry 'Est Charges' (estimated charges) readable. Amount '33 0 0' is visible though the alignment with the £ column is used to confirm the reading.
The name looks like 'Mafsey' but could be 'Massey' or similar; 'for furniture' is legible. Amount '21 5 0' clear in columns but uncertainty in spelling lowers confidence.
Reads 'Whitehead schools' fairly clearly. Amount columns show '103 5 6' — three-digit pounds are well-aligned and readable though the hundreds digit is slightly squeezed.
'Xmas Stipend' clearly written. Amount '5 5 0' aligns well and digits are distinct.
Short name 'Symms Co' (or 'Symm Co') is somewhat unclear; amount appears as '9 15 0' but the first digit could be 7 or 9 due to ink variation—9 chosen based on column alignment.
Large entry reads 'Islington Poor' (left margin note 'Nov.20' nearby). Pounds read as '700' — three digits clearly present though somewhat cramped; unusual large amount so lower confidence.
Entry appears as 'Clerk Wheat' (or 'Clerks Wheat' — singular/plural ambiguous). Amount '10 0 0' is clear and well aligned.
Single-word entry 'Gardener' legible. Amount reads '4 4 2' — the pence '2' is small but visible; column alignment supports reading.
Description 'Servants Party' readable though ink a little faint. Amount appears as '17 16 6' but the tens place of shillings is slightly unclear; chosen reading fits column bounds.
Reads 'Baker Gosford Poor Rate' though the place name could be 'Gosford' or 'Gosforde'. Amount '3 0 0' is small but clear; description spelling uncertain.
Clerical entry 'Rev W Hill' clearly legible. Amount '5 12 6' is visible though the pence 6 is slightly scribbled.
Text reads 'Librarian Ditto paid him' — 'Ditto' indicates same as previous category. Amount '6 6 0' clear though the phrasing is slightly cramped.
Reads 'Poor Rate on Yarnton Tithe' (place name could be 'Yarnton' or similar). Amount '16 5 4' is legible but the middle digits are slightly faint.

Single-word entry 'Stamps' legible. Amount '15 0 0' visible in the columns to the right; alignment supports reading.
Final total at the bottom right reads '1316 1 3' very clearly; ruling lines make the three columns unambiguous. Interpreted as final total for the page.
